name: Apricot Earl Grey Tea Ice Cream
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small heavy saucepan, combine the apricots, 2/3 cup sugar and water. Bring to a boil over moderate heat.
2. Reduce heat to moderately low and simmer, uncovered, until apricots are tender, about 10-12 minutes.
3. Transfer apricots and any remaining liquid to a food processor and puree until smooth, scraping down the sides of the bowl once or twice. Set aside.
4. In a heavy medium saucepan, combine milk and tea leaves. Warm over low heat until milk is hot. Remove from the heat; let steep for 5 minutes. Strain the milk through a fine-meshed strainer.
5. Return milk to the saucepan and add the heavy cream, remaining 1/3 cup sugar and salt. Cook over moderate heat, stirring frequently with a wooden spoon until sugar is completely dissolved and the mixture is hot, about 5-6 minutes. Remove from the heat.
6. In a medium bowl, whisk egg yolks until blended. Gradually whisk in one-third of the hot cream in a thin stream, then whisk the mixture back into the remaining cream in the saucepan.
7. Cook over moderately low heat, stirring constantly, until the custard lightly coats the back of the spoon, about 5-7 minutes. Do not let boil. Immediately remove from the heat and strain the custard into a medium bowl.
8. Cover and refrigerate until cold, at least 6 hours or overnight. Pour custard into an ice cream maker and freeze according to the manufacturer's instructions.
